key_insights.insight: Diet during pregnancy epigenetically programs the baby's DNA, affecting future health risks like diabetes and psychiatric disorders.
key_insights.quote: With your diet during pregnancy, you're programming that DNA. And this is going to have an impact on your baby's development and on his future risk of disease.

key_insights.insight: 90% of pregnant moms are not getting enough choline, which is crucial for baby's brain development, leading to potential lifelong brain deficits.
key_insights.quote: Today, 90% of moms are not getting enough choline during pregnancy... failure to provide choline during this time can result in lifelong brain deficits in the baby.

key_insights.insight: Babies need no fructose during pregnancy, as high glucose levels can lead to epigenetic switches programming them for diabetes, obesity, and psychiatric disorders.
key_insights.quote: Your baby needs no fructose during pregnancy...if you have very high glucose levels during pregnancy, scientists have found that your baby's DNA will have epigenetic switches that are programming him towards having a higher vulnerability to develop diabetes, obesity, and psychiatric disorders.

key_insights.insight: Low protein diets during pregnancy can lead to smaller babies and epigenetic programming towards staying smaller throughout life.
key_insights.quote: Low protein diets lead to smaller babies and potentially this epigenetic programming of staying smaller throughout life.

key_insights.insight: Breast milk is alive with information that continues DNA programming, unlike inert formula; however, formula is nutritionally complete and a viable option.
key_insights.quote: The main difference between breast milk and formula is that breast milk is alive. It's alive with little molecules that are going to continue that DNA programming. Formula is inert. It's not alive. It's not doing that programming.

key_insights.insight: Even low doses of alcohol during pregnancy are suboptimal and may cause facial morphing and weaker connections in brain regions critical for emotional regulation.
key_insights.quote: Even low doses of alcohol cause facial morphing, consistent changes in the shape of the eyes and nose at 12 months persisting up to age 8 and weaker connections in the right anterior singular part of the brain, the region critical for emotional regulation and impulse control.

key_insights.insight: Current "no added sugars" labeling can be deceptive as fruit juices still contain a high amount of naturally occurring sugars.
key_insights.quote: That is so deceptive because this glass of orange juice has no added sugars in it. Because the sugar was there at the beginning. It came from the orange from the original ingredient. So on a can of orange juice, you can say no added sugar.

key_insights.insight: Having your vegetables at the beginning of your meal is incredibly powerful.because vegetables contain fiber and create a mesh to slow down the glucose molecules from carbs.
key_insights.quote: When you have them [vegetables] at the beginning of your meal, they create this protective mesh in your intestine that slows down the glucose molecules from carbs and makes the glucose molecules arrive more slowly into your bloodstream.

key_insights.insight: Mothers who exercised during pregnancy are associated with offspring who solve mazes twice as fast and had fewer anxiety symptoms.
key_insights.quote: The babies that were born to the moms who were exercising solved the maze twice as fast and had fewer anxiety symptoms.

actionable_advice.advice: Eat four eggs a day during pregnancy to ensure sufficient choline intake for the baby's brain development.
actionable_advice.how_to: Incorporate four eggs into your daily diet, perhaps two at breakfast and two as a snack or added to other meals.

actionable_advice.advice: Eat fatty fish three times a week or supplement with 2g of DHA daily to ensure adequate omega-3 intake.
actionable_advice.how_to: Eat fish three times a week and supplement additionally. Good fatty fishes: salmon, sardines, anchovies and mackerel. When supplementing make sure to look for DHA, a very important factor in brain health.

actionable_advice.advice: In the third trimester of pregnancy, consume about 1.6 grams of protein per kilogram of body weight each day.
actionable_advice.how_to: Calculate your protein needs based on your weight, then distribute protein-rich foods such as eggs, fish, meat, Greek yogurt, and protein powder throughout your meals and snacks.

actionable_advice.advice: Engage in regular, moderate exercise during pregnancy, such as 30 minutes of walking or other suitable activities.
actionable_advice.how_to: Incorporate 30 minutes of physical activity each day to improve BDNF levels, helping the baby's brain formation.

actionable_advice.advice: If you have the craving of eating a sweet, remember to eat more protein. That could help with the craving.
actionable_advice.how_to: Before eating that cookie or chocolate, first eat some food that contains protein. That could calm your body and it wouldn't need to look for that sweet in the next time.

memorable_stories: The study of married couples using voodoo dolls to represent annoyance with their spouses found a correlation between glucose lows and more pin pricks.
The story of sugar rations during WWII showing a 15% lower chance of developing diabetes for people who were pregnant in the sugar restriction.
The animal study where pregnant rats who exercised 30 minutes daily had offspring that solved mazes twice as fast with lower anxiety.
contrarian_claims: Fruit is not natural; it's a product of human engineering.
Babies are not set in stone after conception as your diet programs your baby's DNA, not the egg.
Fruit juices should be removed from schools because it is full of sugar.
The myth that you should eat for two during pregnancy.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Pregnancy Diet Expert: The Pregnancy Diet That Rewrites DNA! Why Pregnant Moms Are Being Lied To!
